title: LoaiEletr/rnaseq pipeline parameters
description: A comprehensive RNA-Seq pipeline performing upstream analysis, differential exon/isoform usage, differential gene expression, WGCNA co-expression analysis, and functional enrichment

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

genome.help_text:
If using a reference genome configured in the pipeline, use this parameter to give the ID for the reference. This is then used to build the full paths for all required reference genome files e.g. `--genome GRCh38`. 

See the [nf-core website docs](https://nf-co.re/usage/reference_genomes) for more details.

species.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory*. Specifies the species for reference genome lookup. Example: --species human --genome GRCh38. Available species: human, mouse, rat, yeast, fruitfly, zebrafish, worm, arabidopsis, chicken, cow, pig, dog, monkey

fasta.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if --genome is not specified. Path to reference genome FASTA file (.fa.gz, .fasta.gz).

gtf.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--genome` is not specified.

bed.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--gtf` is not specified for RSeQC analysis.

transcriptome.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if --genome is not specified. Path to transcriptome FASTA file for kallisto/salmon (.fasta.gz, .fa.gz).

gtf_isoform.description: Path to extended GTF (.gz) for isoform analysis.
gtf_isoform.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if --genome is not specified. Extended GTF with isoform annotations for differential isoform usage.

contaminant_species.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if --skip_bbsplit false. Direct path to contaminant genome FASTA file. Usage: --contaminant_species mouse.

contaminant_fasta.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if --contaminant_species is not specified. Direct path to contaminant genome FASTA file.

rrna_db.help_text: This parameter is mandatory if --skip_sortmerna false. SortMeRNA rRNA database URL or local path.

reference_base.help_text: Base URL for downloading Ensembl reference files. Used when auto-fetching genome/annotation files.

igenomes_base.description: The base path to the igenomes reference files

igenomes_ignore.description: Do not load the iGenomes reference config.
igenomes_ignore.help_text: Do not load `igenomes.config` when running the pipeline. You may choose this option if you observe clashes between custom parameters and those supplied in `igenomes.config`.

pre_built_indices_options.title: Pre-built Indices Options
pre_built_indices_options.description: Paths to pre-built index files for various tools. If provided, these will be used instead of building indices from scratch.

save_reference.description: If generated by the pipeline save the indicies in the results directory.
save_reference.help_text: If the pipeline generates an alignment index, use this parameter to save it to your results folder for future pipeline runs, reducing processing time.

bbsplit_index.description: Path to pre-built Bbsplit index folder or .tar.gz archive.
bbsplit_index.help_text: The BBSplit index must be built at least once with this pipeline. Use `--save_reference` to save the index, which can then be provided via `--bbsplit_index` for future runs.

sortmerna_index.description: Path to pre-built SortMeRNA index folder or .tar.gz archive.
sortmerna_index.help_text: The SortMeRNA index must be built at least once with this pipeline. Use `--save_reference` to save the index, which can then be provided via `--sortmerna_index` for future runs.

hisat2_index.description: Path to pre-built Hisat2 index folder or .tar.gz archive.
hisat2_index.help_text: The Hisat2 index must be built at least once with this pipeline. Use `--save_reference` to save the index, which can then be provided via `--hisat2_index` for future runs.

kallisto_index.description: Path to pre-built Kallisto index folder or .tar.gz archive.
kallisto_index.help_text: The Kallisto index must be built at least once with this pipeline. Use `--save_reference` to save the index, which can then be provided via `--kallisto_index` for future runs.

salmon_index.description: Path to pre-built Salmon index folder or .tar.gz archive
salmon_index.help_text: The Salmon index must be built at least once with this pipeline. Use `--save_reference` to save the index, which can then be provided via `--salmon_index` for future runs.

analysis_selection_quality_control.title: Analysis Selection & Quality Control
analysis_selection_quality_control.description: Core pipeline options to enable/disable specific analysis modules and quality control steps.

with_umi.help_text: Set to true for UMI-based protocols

skip_umi_extract.help_text: Skip UMI extraction (only relevant if --with_umi true).

adapters.help_text:
Path to FASTA file containing adapter sequences to trim. If not provided, the pipeline will automatically:
- Detect whether data is single-end or paired-end from the input samplesheet
- Use default adapter files from the pipeline's assets folder:
  * For paired-end data: `assets/adapters-PE.fa`
  * For single-end data: `assets/adapters-SE.fa`

remove_duplicates.description: Physically remove duplicate reads from the BAM file
remove_duplicates.help_text: If true, duplicate reads will be removed rather than just marked. This is passed to Picard MarkDuplicates via the REMOVE_DUPLICATES=true argument.

skip_picard_markduplicates.description: Skip identification and marking of duplicate reads

skip_baserecalibration.description: Skip GATK Base Quality Score Recalibration (BQSR)

skip_variantfiltration.description: Skip GATK hard-filtering of variant calls

skip_interval_splitting.description: Skip splitting of intervals for parallel processing
skip_interval_splitting.help_text: By default, the pipeline splits the interval list into smaller chunks to run GATK tools in parallel across multiple CPUs. Setting this to true will process the entire interval list in a single job.

umi_processing.title: UMI Processing Options
umi_processing.description: Options for Unique Molecular Identifier (UMI) extraction and deduplication.

lib_kit.help_text: This parameter is mandatory if with_umi true. Kit type: 'quantseq', 'corall', 'takara'.

umitools_bc_pattern.help_text: Overrides the default pattern provided by --lib_kit. Example: '^(?P<umi_1>.{8}).*'

umi_separator.description: Separator used to append UMI to read name.

alignment_quantification.title: Alignment & Quantification
alignment_quantification.description: Options for read alignment, transcript quantification, and library-specific processing.

pseudo_aligner.help_text: This parameter is mandatory for transcript quantification. Options: 'kallisto' or 'salmon'.

fragment_length.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* for single-end reads when using kallisto as the pseudo_aligner.

fragment_length_sd.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* for single-end reads when using kallisto as the pseudo_aligner.

bootstrap_count.help_text: This parameter is *optional* for either single-end or paired-end reads when using kallisto as the pseudo_aligner.

statistical_thresholds.title: Statistical Thresholds
statistical_thresholds.description: Statistical cutoffs and thresholds applied across multiple analysis types

pvalue_threshold.help_text: Global P-value threshold for statistical significance. Applied to: differential gene expression, exon usage, isoform usage, alternative splicing, GO enrichment, KEGG enrichment, and other statistical tests.

logfc_threshold.help_text: Global log2 fold change threshold for biological effect size. Applied to: differential expression and exon usage filtering.

ntop_genes.help_text: Number of top-ranking genes to display in results. Applied to: (1) Differential expression analysis: displays top N differentially expressed genes, (2) Exon usage analysis: displays top N genes with significant differential exon usage.

differential_expression_analysis.title: Differential Expression Analysis
differential_expression_analysis.description: Parameters controlling differential gene expression detection, statistical thresholds, and result reporting.

cluster_method.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when diffexpr_method is set to masigpro (for timecourse analysis). Clustering method: 'hclust', 'kmeans', 'Mclust'.

rsq_threshold.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when diffexpr_method is set to masigpro (for timecourse analysis).

differential_exon_usage_analysis.title: Differential Exon Usage Analysis
differential_exon_usage_analysis.description: Parameters for detecting differential exon usage between conditions, focusing on exon-level expression changes.

gff.description: Path to GFF annotation file for preparing annotation for DEXSeq analysis.
gff.help_text: The GFF file is used to prepare annotation for DEXSeq. Use `--save_reference` to save the annotation, which can then be provided via `--gff` for future runs.

wgcna_network_analysis.title: WGCNA Network Analysis
wgcna_network_analysis.description: Parameters for weighted gene co-expression network analysis, module detection, and hub gene identification.

min_gs.description: Minimum gene significance for module detection.
min_gs.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

min_mm.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

ntop_hubgenes.description: Number of hub genes to identify per module.
ntop_hubgenes.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

cor_threshold.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

tomtype.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA. TOM type: 'none', 'signed', 'signed 2', 'unsigned', 'unsigned 2', 'signed Nowick', or 'signed Nowick 2'

networktype.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA. Network type: 'signed', 'unsigned', or 'signed hybrid'

deepsplit.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA. Module splitting depth (0-4).

reassignthreshold.description: Threshold for gene reassignment between modules.
reassignthreshold.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

mergecutheight.description: Height threshold for merging similar modules.
mergecutheight.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

minmodulesize.description: Minimum number of genes per module.
minmodulesize.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

sft_r2_threshold.description: R² threshold for scale-free topology fit during soft-threshold selection.
sft_r2_threshold.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

score_threshold.description: Confidence score threshold for filtering STRING PPI interactions.
score_threshold.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes WGCNA.

isoform_splicing_analysis.title: Isoform & Splicing Analysis
isoform_splicing_analysis.description: Settings for differential isoform usage, alternative splicing detection, and transcript-level analysis.

dif_cutoff.description: Differential usage cutoff for isoform switching.
dif_cutoff.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes DIU.

ntop_isoforms.description: Number of top differentially used isoforms in reports.
ntop_isoforms.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes DIU.

statoff.description: Disable statistical testing in splicing analysis.
statoff.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

novelss.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

allowclipping.description: Allow clipped alignments in splicing analysis.
allowclipping.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

individualcounts.description: Output read counts per splicing event.
individualcounts.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

event_types.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

delta_psi.description: Delta Percent Spliced In threshold for significant events.
delta_psi.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* when analysis_method includes AS.

functional_enrichment_analysis.title: Functional Enrichment Analysis
functional_enrichment_analysis.description: Settings for gene set enrichment, pathway analysis, and biological interpretation of results.

enrichment_method.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* for performing functional enrichment. Options: 'GO', 'KEGG', 'GSEA'. Usage: --enrichment_method KEGG,GO

nes_threshold.description: Normalized Enrichment Score threshold for GSEA.

msigdb_categories.help_text: Comma-separated MSigDB categories (H, C1-C8) for enrichment.

ntop_processes.help_text:
Number of top enriched processes to display in reports. Includes:
- **GO**: Biological Process (BP), Molecular Function (MF), Cellular Component (CC)
- **KEGG**: Pathway enrichment
- **GSEA**: Hallmark (H1) and Collections (C1-C8), particularly C5 (GO: BP, MF, CC)

rank_method.help_text:
GSEA ranking method. Options:
- `logfc`: Rank genes by log fold change (logFC)
- `t_stat`: Rank genes by t-statistic
- `signed_significance`: Rank genes by signed significance (logFC × -log10(p-value))

germline_variant_calling.title: Germline Variant Calling Analysis
germline_variant_calling.description: Parameters for GATK4-based variant discovery, recalibration, and annotation

fai.description: Path to Samtools Fasta Index (.fai) file
fai.help_text: Enables fast random access to the reference FASTA.

dict.description: Path to GATK Sequence Dictionary (.dict) file
dict.help_text: Contains sequence metadata required by GATK tools.

interval_list.description: Path to GATK Interval List (.interval_list) file
interval_list.help_text: Defines genomic regions for targeted variant calling (e.g., exome capture targets).

dbsnp.description: Path to VCF file containing known SNPs (e.g., dbSNP)
dbsnp.help_text: Used for variant annotation. Must be a VCF file (e.g., 'dbsnp_146.hg38.vcf.gz').

dbsnp_tbi.description: Path to VCF index for the dbSNP file
dbsnp_tbi.help_text: Required for indexing the dbSNP VCF.

knownsites.description: Path to VCF file(s) containing known indels for BQSR
knownsites.help_text: Comma-separated list of VCFs used to mask known indels during recalibration.

knownsites_tbi.description: Path to VCF index file(s) for the known sites
knownsites_tbi.help_text: Must match the files provided in `--knownsites`.

snpeff_db.description: Path to SnpEff database folder or tar archive
snpeff_db.help_text: Path to SnpEff database. If not provided, SnpEff will attempt to download based on `--snpeff_genome`.

snpeff_genome.help_text: The specific SnpEff database version to use for annotation (e.g., 'GRCm39.99').

gatk_interval_scatter_count.description: Number of intervals to scatter for parallel processing
gatk_interval_scatter_count.help_text: Determines how many chunks the interval list is split into for parallelized GATK execution. Increasing this number increases parallelism but also overhead.

gatk_hc_call_conf.help_text: The minimum phred-scaled confidence threshold at which variants should be called. Variants with a score below this will be filtered out.

gatk_vf_window_size.description: Window size for GATK VariantFiltration cluster analysis
gatk_vf_window_size.help_text: Defines the window size (in base pairs) to evaluate clusters of variants during hard filtering.

gatk_vf_cluster_size.description: Maximum number of variants allowed within the specified window
gatk_vf_cluster_size.help_text: If more than this number of variants are found within the window size, they are filtered out as a cluster.

gatk_vf_fs_filter.help_text: Phred-scaled p-value using Fisher's Exact Test to detect strand bias. Higher values indicate more bias. GATK standard for RNA-seq is typically 30.0.

gatk_vf_qd_filter.help_text: Variant confidence normalized by depth. Low QD values suggest the call is likely an artifact.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
